M-046.NAME: krotpn-domain-tls-edge
M-046.path: backend/app/core/config.py nginx docker-compose.yml .env.example deploy/haproxy-phase38.cfg deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml mtproto-runtime scripts docs/edge/PHASE-32-CUTOVER.xml docs/plans/Phase-32.xml docs/plans/Phase-37.xml docs/plans/Phase-38.xml
M-046.owns: krotpn.xyz domain edge, HTTP to HTTPS redirect, public admin 8443, wildcard TLS expectations, and shared 443 routing contract for HTTPS plus MTProto.
M-046.purpose: Define the production edge model needed for https://krotpn.xyz and Telegram fake-TLS MTProto proxy to coexist safely.

exports.edge-http-redirect: Force http://krotpn.xyz and http://www.krotpn.xyz to https://krotpn.xyz.
exports.edge-wildcard-tls: Use trusted certificates covering krotpn.xyz and *.krotpn.xyz.
exports.edge-shared-443: Route MTProto fake-TLS by issued SNI and forward normal HTTPS to KrotPN frontend/backend while keeping the admin panel on public 8443.
exports.edge-ru-sni-router: Phase-38 public 443 router sends web SNI to RU nginx fallback and issued MTProto SNI to the DE runtime.
exports.edge-static-guard: Static checks proving deploy/install changes stay scoped to explicitly approved Phase-35 wildcard TLS installer work.

edge-policy.policy-1: Shared 443 design must be verified before replacing direct nginx ownership of 443.
edge-policy.policy-2: Wildcard certificate issuance may remain operator-driven; this module defines runtime expectations first.
edge-policy.policy-3: Existing deploy/install scripts are protected unless the operator explicitly approves edge deployment changes; Phase-35 is the approved installer wildcard TLS exception.
edge-policy.policy-4: Phase-37 makes mtproto-edge the production TCP 443 owner and moves nginx HTTPS fallback to 8443.
edge-policy.policy-5: Phase-38 supersedes the RU-only 443 owner model with an RU SNI router plus DE-backed MTProto runtime after Phase-37 live blocking.
edge-policy.policy-6: Phase-38 public admin access stays on 8443; the SNI-router web fallback uses private loopback 9443.

constraints.constraint-1: Misrouting 443 can break both website and Telegram proxy, so rollout requires a rollback plan.
constraints.constraint-2: Self-signed certificates are unacceptable for production Telegram fake-TLS links.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-14: Phase-32 local static verification passed for settings, nginx redirect/fallback, compose config, protected deploy/install guard, cutover runbook, XML parse, and MyGRACE lint.
evidence.evidence-2: Production smoke remains blocked-live until operator-provided DNS, trusted wildcard certificate, and rollback window are available.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-14: Phase-34 release handoff records V-M-046 as operator-live and keeps deploy/install/edge surfaces protected during local stabilization.
evidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-17: Phase-35 added deploy-supported operator wildcard TLS reuse and runtime nginx domain rendering while preserving M-046 shared-443 live gate.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-18: Phase-37 corrected the clean-VPS live failure where nginx owned public 443 and no MTProto edge was running; nginx now listens on 8443 behind mtproto-edge.
evidence.evidence-6: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 plan changes the intended public 443 owner to an RU SNI router so HTTPS fallback and DE MTProto runtime can coexist.
evidence.evidence-7: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 local execution added HAProxy SNI router config and compose wiring so public 443 is owned by krotpn-sni-router while nginx stays on 80/9443 private fallback, 8443 public admin, and 8080 legacy admin.
evidence.evidence-8: 2026-05-18: Clean VPS deploy exposed two live edge regressions: HAProxy could not bind public 443 without explicit low-port permissions, and direct https://krotpn.xyz:8443/login showed the user frontend until admin 8443 was separated from private fallback 9443.

M-046.hotspot: H-032: Shared 443 edge can break both HTTPS and Telegram proxy if fallback routing or wildcard TLS is misconfigured
